title: Tapinanthus belvisii
taxonomy: Tapinanthus belvisii belongs to the family Loranthaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as mistletoes. The Loranthaceae family is a part of the order Santalales. Tapinanthus belvisii is a genus of mistletoes that is native to tropical Africa. This plant is a type of hemiparasitic species, which means it obtains some of its nutrients by parasitizing other plants. The classification of Tapinanthus belvisii further includes its kingdom as Plantae and its class as Magnoliopsida.
